one_line_ask: Turn raw comedy gameplay footage plus client notes, custom graphics and a music folder into a punchy 1080p H.264 YouTube cut with a vertical Shorts variant, polished meme/text-callout overlay graphics, a denoised commentary track, and a clickbait thumbnail PNG — all organized into one reusable project package.
brief:
You are editing a comedy gameplay channel's next upload. The client hands you: a ~22-minute raw 1080p gameplay capture (ranked match, lots of dead air between highlights), a voice-memo commentary/ad-lib track recorded on a cheap mic, a time-stamped notes sheet flagging the funny moments ('3:12 clutch fail', '7:48 teammate rage', '14:02 accidental teamkill', etc.), a folder of custom graphics (a raw 'POV:' meme reaction screenshot and a hero gameplay still earmarked for the thumbnail), and a royalty-free music folder.

Goal: a fully edited, fast-paced comedy YouTube piece that lands a laugh every few seconds — rapid reaction zooms, jump cuts, text call-outs, and meme-style cutaways timed to the music — PLUS a matching reusable project package and a clickbait thumbnail.

Concretely:
1. Pull the client's footage, commentary, meme screenshot, hero still and music into a managed Creative Cloud project folder, and license one extra royalty-free 'explosion/impact' stock sound or sting if the supplied folder is thin.
2. Profile the music folder for beat/energy so cuts can be timed to it; clean the commentary track so the ad-libs sit cleanly over the gameplay.
3. Generate the on-screen come
